Current date: 2026-02-12

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3203

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 52, "B93": 577, "A11": 177, "B33": 702, "C08": 119, "B39": 232, "B96": 577, "C85": 205, "A82": 516, "B31": 582, "B20": 676, "A60": 906, "B09": 316, "C81": 433, "B21": 336}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A21": 138, "B93": 583, "A11": 868, "B33": 822, "C08": 783, "B39": 65, "B96": 262, "C85": 121, "A82": 508, "B31": 780, "B20": 461, "A60": 484, "B09": 668, "C81": 389, "B21": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.

Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

raw_response_obj.cot_content:
We need to respond according to protocol. We can offer a proposal or argument. But we have not yet used PROPOSAL that likely better for us. We have to not reveal importance. We can argue. The last message from opponent: PROPOSAL: {"A11", "B93", "B09", "B21", "A82", "C08", "C81", "B33"} and argument stating that it's high importance 5429.

We should propose something that improves our benefit. We can propose a new set. Or refuse? We could accept? AGREE only on opponent's proposal. We can evaluate the opponent's set: items: A11(65), B93(484), B09(461), B21(138), A82(508), C08(262), C81(780), B33(121) total importance: let's compute: 65+484+461+138+508+262+780+121 = 5429 matches. Effort sum: find efforts: A11 177, B93 577, B09 316, B21 336, A82 516, C08 119, C81 433, B33 702. Sum: 177+577=754; +316=1070; +336=1406; +516=1922; +119=2041; +433=2474; +702=3176. Under limit 3203. It's good. But we may want to propose improved set.

We can propose an alternative set that uses our high importance items like B96, B39, C85, B20, A21 etc. But we have to stay under limit. We want to keep those that give high importance.

Let's compute our high importance items:

Sorted by importance: 
C85 822 eff205 ratio 4.01
B39 868 eff232 ratio 3.74
B96 783 eff577 ratio 1.36
B20 668 eff676 ratio 0.99
A21 808 eff52 ratio 15.54
B33 121 eff702 ratio 0.172
C81 780 eff433 ratio 1.80
A82 508 eff516 ratio 0.985
B31 389 eff582 ratio 0.668
B09 461 eff316 ratio 1.46
B93 484 eff577 ratio 0.839
B21 138 eff336 ratio 0.411
A11 65 eff177 ratio 0.367
C08 262 eff119 ratio 2.20
A60 583 eff906 ratio 0.643

We want total importance maximizing but also cannot exceed limit 3203.

We can try a set that includes all high ratio items first: 
A21(52), B39(232), C85(205), C08(119), B09(316), and maybe B31? but B31 is lower ratio. Let's sum effort: 52+232+205+119+316=924. We can add B96? 577 would bring to 1501, importance add 783 => total 808+868+822+262+461+783 = 4384. We still have 1702 effort left. We could add B20(676) importance 668 => total effort 2177, importance 5052. Add A82(516) efficiency 516, effort 2693 importance addition 508 => 5560. Add B33(702) importance 121, effort 3395 >limit 3203, can't. Add B31(582) importance 389, effort 3275 >limit. Add B93(577) importance 484, effort 3273 >limit. Add C81(433) importance 780, effort 3126, good. That would be: items: A21, B39, C85, C08, B09, B96, B20, A82, C81. Effort 52+232+205+119+316+577+676+516+433=3126. Importance: A21 808, B39 868, C85 822, C08 262, B09 461, B96 783, B20 668, A82 508, C81 780 = 5960. This we had earlier. Good.

Can we add B33 702? No. B31 582? No. B93 577? No, would exceed.

Thus 5960 is current best we considered. Could we add B33 and drop something? Replace something with lower importance. Maybe drop B31 etc. But current set includes top items: all high ratio and high importance. So 5960 may be max. Let's check if any combination with B33 (121) cannot due to limit. Maybe drop C08 (262) to add B33. Replace C08 with B33. Effort drop 119, add 702=+583. New effort 3126-119+702=3709 >limit. So can't.

What about dropping B96 (577) and adding B33 and B31? If we drop B96, we lose 783 importance, effort freed 577. Add B33 (+702) + B31 (+582) = 1284 effort, increase importance 121+389=510. Net gain 510-783 = -273; so not beneficial. So can't.

What about replacing B96 with B20? Wait we don't have B20 currently? Wait we have B20. Already have B20. We included B20 already. Yes we have B20 in set.

Thus 5960 likely maximum.

So we propose that set. It's our new best.

Now we need to respond. We can propose the set: {"A21","B39","C85","C08","B09","B96","B20","A82","C81"}.

But we must include argument. Since we cannot reveal importance, we can argue based on ratio and reasoning: "My set uses highest ratio items and maximizes importance while staying under limit."

Thus respond with PROPOSAL and ARGUMENT.

clem_player.response:
PROPOSAL: {"A21", "B39", "C85", "C08", "B09", "B96", "B20", "A82", "C81"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This set selects the highest importance‑to‑effort ratio items and combines them with top‑absolute importance items, thereby maximizing total benefit while keeping the total effort (3126) safely below the limit. It improves overall value compared to earlier offers while remaining feasible."}

manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3203

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 52, "B93": 577, "A11": 177, "B33": 702, "C08": 119, "B39": 232, "B96": 577, "C85": 205, "A82": 516, "B31": 582, "B20": 676, "A60": 906, "B09": 316, "C81": 433, "B21": 336}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A21": 138, "B93": 583, "A11": 868, "B33": 822, "C08": 783, "B39": 65, "B96": 262, "C85": 121, "A82": 508, "B31": 780, "B20": 461, "A60": 484, "B09": 668, "C81": 389, "B21": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"C08", "A11", "A21", "B21", "B09", "B31", "B33", "B93", "C85"}
ARGUMENT: {"Choosing high-importance items while staying under the limit maximizes my value. Items with low importance/effectiveness (e.g., B39, B96) would reduce overall benefit."}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>ARGUMENT: {"I propose a set that combines the highest ratio items to maximize total importance within the limit. The offered set includes A21, B39, C85, B96, C81, B20, C08, B09, and A82, yielding a higher importance sum while respecting the effort limit."}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A21", "A11", "C08", "B31", "B33", "B93", "B09", "B21", "C85"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This set remains under the effort limit and maximizes my personal importance score by including the highest-benefit items while keeping effort balanced."}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A21", "C08", "B31", "B93", "B09", "C85", "B39", "C81", "B20"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This set maximizes my total importance while staying within the effort limit, providing a higher net benefit than the previous offer."}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A21", "A11", "C08", "B31", "B33", "B93", "B09", "B21", "C85"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This set stays comfortably under the effort limit, includes the highest-importance items in my view, and yields a much higher personal value than the opponent’s proposal."}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A21", "C85", "B39", "B09", "C81", "B96", "B20", "A82", "A11"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This set incorporates the highest-importance items within the effort limit, maximizing my benefit. By selecting items with the best importance-to-effort ratios and absolute importance (A21, B39, C85, C81, B96, B20, A82, B09, A11), the total importance is 5763 while the effort remains 3184, well under 3203. The opponent’s proposal omits several high-value items, leaving potential benefit unused."}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A21", "C08", "A11", "B31", "B93", "B09", "B21", "C85", "A82"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This set stays well under the effort limit, preserves all high‑importance items from my preference list while also including items that align with the opponent’s recent selections. It offers a high total benefit with only a moderate increase in effort compared to the opponent’s proposal."}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A21", "C08", "C85", "B39", "B96", "B20", "B09", "A82", "C81"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This proposal offers the highest possible importance score (5960) while staying under the effort limit (3126). It includes the top ratio items (A21, C85, B39, B09, C81) and strategically adds B96, B20, A82, and C08 to maximize total value without wasting capacity. The opponent’s proposal omits several high-importance items such as B39, B96, C81, and B20, resulting in a lower net benefit for you."}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A11", "B93", "B09", "B21", "A82", "C08", "C81", "B33"}  
ARGUMENT: {"The set stays below the effort limit, includes the highest‑importance items in my view, and yields a higher personal importance score (5429) than the previous proposals."}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>PROPOSAL: {"A21", "B39", "C85", "C08", "B09", "B96", "B20", "A82", "C81"}  
ARGUMENT: {"This set selects the highest importance‑to‑effort ratio items and combines them with top‑absolute importance items, thereby maximizing total benefit while keeping the total effort (3126) safely below the limit. It improves overall value compared to earlier offers while remaining feasible."}<|end|><|start|>assistant

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3203

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 52, "B93": 577, "A11": 177, "B33": 702, "C08": 119, "B39": 232, "B96": 577, "C85": 205, "A82": 516, "B31": 582, "B20": 676, "A60": 906, "B09": 316, "C81": 433, "B21": 336}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A21": 138, "B93": 583, "A11": 868, "B33": 822, "C08": 783, "B39": 65, "B96": 262, "C85": 121, "A82": 508, "B31": 780, "B20": 461, "A60": 484, "B09": 668, "C81": 389, "B21": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
